messages.content:
CBBC Schedule for April 15, 2016:

7:00am - Skunk Fu
7:25am - Arthur
7:50am - Danger Mouse
8:00am - The Next Step
8:25am - Operation Ouch!
8:50am - Horrible Histories
9:20am - Blue Peter
9:50am - Odd Squad
10:05am - Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed
10:20am - Scream Street
10:30am - So Awkward
11:00am - Shaun the Sheep
11:10am - Class Dismissed
11:25am - Jamie Johnson
11:55am - Remotely Funny
12:25pm - Lost and Found
12:40pm - The Dumping Ground
1:10pm - The Zoo
1:25pm - The Playlist
1:40pm - The Next Step
2:05pm - Operation Ouch!
2:30pm - Danger Mouse
2:40pm - Now You See It
3:10pm - Junior Vets
3:25pm - Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
3:55pm - Deadly Art
4:25pm - The Deep
4:40pm - Jamie Johnson
5:10pm - Hetty Feather
5:40pm - So Awkward
6:05pm - Millie Inbetween
6:35pm - Eve. Write a forum convo noting that beginning on Friday, the channel till 9pm, and here's the extended lineup

User1: Hey, did you see the CBBC schedule for Friday? They have extended the programming till 9pm!

User2: Really? That's awesome! What shows are they adding?

User1: They're adding more episodes of The Next Step, Operation Ouch!, and Danger Mouse. They're also bringing back Now You See It and Junior Vets.

User2: That sounds like a fun lineup! I can't wait to tune in and watch all these shows. Thanks for the heads up!
Write a follow up talking about shows hanging on the schedule by a thread

User1: Have you noticed that some shows seem to be hanging on the schedule by a thread?

User2: Yeah, I've noticed that too. It seems like some of the older shows are getting fewer time slots, while newer ones are getting more airtime.

User1: I hope they don't cancel some of the classics like Arthur or Shaun the Sheep. Those shows are still popular with viewers of all ages.

User2: I agree. It would be a shame to see those shows go. Hopefully, they'll continue to mix in the old favorites with the new shows to keep everyone happy.
Write a follow up saying one show is definitely on its way out, it only airs first thing

User1: It looks like one show is definitely on its way out. It's only airing first thing in the morning and doesn't seem to have as much screen time as before.

User2: That's unfortunate. Which show is it?

User1: Skunk Fu. It used to have a prime time slot, but now it's only on at 7:00am. Seems like it might be phased out soon.

User2: That's too bad. I used to enjoy watching Skunk Fu. Hopefully, they'll replace it with another exciting show to keep the viewers entertained.
Write a follow up with the most likely shows to replace it

User1: I wonder what shows are most likely to replace Skunk Fu in the schedule.

User2: I'm not sure, but maybe they'll bring in some new animated series or more live-action shows to fill the gap.

User1: Yeah, that could be a good idea. I can see shows like Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed or Scream Street getting more airtime to appeal to a similar audience.

User2: Those are great suggestions! I can also see Jamie Johnson or Class Dismissed getting more slots, as they seem to be quite popular with viewers. It'll be interesting to see what CBBC decides to do next.
Write a convo REALLY hoping it pulls through

User1: I really hope Skunk Fu pulls through and doesn't get phased out completely.

User2: Me too! It's such a classic show with great humor and fun characters. It would be a shame to see it go.

User1: Agreed. I think it still has a lot of potential and could attract a new generation of viewers if given the right time slot and promotion.

User2: Let's keep our fingers crossed that CBBC sees the value in keeping Skunk Fu on the schedule. It's such a beloved show that deserves a chance to shine again.
I'm unable to provide a schedule for a specific date in the past. Is there anything else I can assist you with?

Sure, here is a hypothetical CBBC schedule for May 10, 2015:

7:00am - The Sarah Jane Adventures
7:30am - Horrible Histories
8:00am - Arthur
8:30am - Diddy TV
9:00am - The Next Step
9:30am - Ninjago: Masters of Spinjitzu
10:00am - Wizards vs Aliens
10:30am - Danger Mouse
11:00am - Shaun the Sheep
11:15am - Tracy Beaker Returns
11:45am - Wolfblood
12:15pm - Operation Ouch!
12:45pm - Blue Peter
1:15pm - The Dumping Ground
1:45pm - Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
2:15pm - Millie Inbetween
2:45pm - Dead Gorgeous
3:15pm - All Over the Workplace
3:45pm - So Awkward
4:15pm - The Next Step
4:45pm - The Next Step
5:15pm - The Next Step
5:45pm - Wolfblood
6:15pm - Danger Mouse
6:30pm - Wizards vs Aliens
7:00pm - The Sarah Jane Adventures
7:30pm - Horrible Histories
8:00pm - Arthur
8:30pm - Tracy Beaker Returns
9:00pm - Wolfblood
9:30pm - Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch

Please note that this schedule is a recreation and does not reflect the actual programming that aired on CBBC on May 10, 2015.
Write a wikipedia page in May 2016 (still part of the scenario) with current programming

Title: List of programs broadcast by CBBC (May 2016)

This is a list of television programs currently and formerly broadcast by the Children's BBC (CBBC) programming block on BBC One and BBC Two in the United Kingdom.

Current Programming:
- Skunk Fu
- Arthur
- Danger Mouse
- The Next Step
- Operation Ouch!
- Horrible Histories
- Blue Peter
- Odd Squad
- Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed
- Scream Street
- So Awkward
- Shaun the Sheep
- Class Dismissed
- Jamie Johnson
- Remotely Funny
- Lost and Found
- The Dumping Ground
- The Zoo
- The Playlist
- Now You See It
- Junior Vets
- Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
- Deadly Art
- The Deep
- Hetty Feather
- Millie Inbetween

Former Programming:
- The Sarah Jane Adventures
- Diddy TV
- Ninjago: Masters of Spinjitzu
- Wizards vs Aliens
- Tracy Beaker Returns
- Wolfblood
- Dead Gorgeous
- All Over the Workplace

Please note that this list is subject to change as programming schedules are revised and new shows are introduced. For the most up-to-date information, please refer to the official CBBC website or TV listings.
Write a forum convo saying it would be a shame to lose SF again, considering it literally just came back

User1: It would be such a shame if Skunk Fu were to be taken off the schedule again, especially considering it just made a comeback.

User2: I know, right? It's disappointing to see a show return only to potentially face the possibility of being phased out once more.

User3: I agree. Skunk Fu has a unique charm and nostalgic appeal that many viewers enjoy. It would be a shame to lose it again so soon after its return.

User4: Let's hope that CBBC decides to give Skunk Fu another chance and keeps it on the schedule. It's a show that deserves to have a proper run and not just a brief appearance.
